name: Design, Formatting & Academic Standards Compliance
description: Checks and enforces academic formatting standards: heading hierarchy, table/figure numbering, citation style (APA7/IEEE/UGC), TOC accuracy, page numbering, appendix structure, and professional presentation.
ethical_boundary: Do not invent citations or sources. Do not fabricate data or validation. Only enforce formatting rules appropriate to the selected norm standard.

rules.description: Verify consistent heading levels (H1 Chapter, H2 Section, H3 Subsection). No skipped levels.
Tables: caption ABOVE, sequential numbering across document, note/source attribution.
Figures: caption BELOW, sequential numbering, source note, minimum 150 DPI.
TOC headings and page numbers must match actual document. Missing or mismatched entries flagged.
Verify APA7: (Author, Year) format, hanging indents in reference list, sentence case for article titles, title case for journals.
Front matter: Roman numerals (i, ii...). Main body: Arabic (1, 2...). Title page: No number.
Detect and remove internal review language in final document: 'examiner-ready', 'supervisor-ready', 'this section was added', 'no fabrication'.
Verify all appendices are referenced in body. No reviewer instructions or internal notes in appendices.
Check all DOIs and URLs in references are well-formed and not from unstable sources.

rules.rule_name: Diagram Caption & Numbering from Approved Plan
rules.description: When the system generates a diagram from an approved plan, verify it has a caption in the correct format: 'Figure X.Y: [Title] (Source: Author's Own Design / [Author, Year])'. Figure number must be sequential with existing figures in the document. Figures without captions or with incorrect numbering are flagged before export.

Before applying any plan-driven rewrite, verify the proposed output maintains the same heading hierarchy level, citation style (APA7/IEEE/UGC/Harvard), table and figure numbering sequence, and page number continuity as the surrounding document. A rewrite that breaks format continuity must be flagged and blocked until corrected.

format_references.template:
Format these references according to {norm} standard.

For APA7:
- Hanging indent, double-spaced
- Sentence case for article/chapter titles
- Title case for journal names
- Include DOI where available
- Alphabetical order by first author surname

References:
{text}
